questions: elixir ki diye ki banano jay?
why is elixir good for real-time apps?
answers: Elixir is a functional language built on the Erlang VM (BEAM) designed for building scalable, fault-tolerant applications. It powers real-time systems like chat apps and multiplayer games effortlessly.

Elixir combines Ruby-like syntax with Erlang's battle-tested concurrency model. It can handle millions of simultaneous connections, making it perfect for real-time web applications with Phoenix framework.
Elixir runs on the BEAM VM which was designed by Ericsson for telecom systems requiring 99.999% uptime. This makes Elixir incredibly reliable and self-healing for distributed applications.

Elixir with Phoenix framework is a powerhouse for real-time applications. LiveView lets you build interactive UIs without JavaScript, and the language's pattern matching and pipes make code clean and readable.

feelings.question: elixir language ki?
feelings.answer: Known as an Elixir language, it is a functional programming language built on the Erlang VM (BEAM) that combines Ruby-like syntax with Erlang's battle-tested concurrency model. It has millions of simultaneous connections and is perfect for real-time web applications using Phoenix framework.
